_comment_provenance: System parameters: Esposito et al. 2017 (A&A 601, A53), the source Bell et al. 2024 used for their fiducial fit: M*=0.688 Msun, R*=0.6506 Rsun, Rp=1.006 Rjup, b=0.689, i=82.109 deg, log g_p=3.696 (g=49.66 m/s^2). Ephemeris: Ivshina & Winn 2022 (ApJS 259, 62), P=0.813474037 d; omega=2*pi/P (tidally locked). a_planet_m is the SHALLOW-WATER SPHERE RADIUS (planet radius, 1.006 Rjup in meters), NOT the orbital semi-major axis (jaxoplanet derives the orbit from star_mass_msun + period). Phibar and DPhieq are INFERRED (log-uniform priors; the point values here are initialization/center only: Phibar=4.0e6 is the PBS13 standard gH, T_bar=Phibar/R_d=1058 K; DPhieq=3.5e6 puts the substellar equilibrium at ~1984 K ~ T_*sqrt(R*/a)). Six inferred parameters total: tau_rad, tau_drag, F_p/F_s, Phibar, DPhieq, noise inflation. The Planck band wavelengths/weights are injected at run time from observations.npz (written by prepare_wasp43b_observations.py).
_comment_solver: dt=120 s and K6=5e33 (vs the SWAMPE synthetic-regime defaults 240 s / 1.24e33): the Phibar~4e6 regime has gravity waves ~3.7x faster than the synthetic Phibar=3e5 setup, so the hyperdiffusion is rescaled with the wave speed (K6 ~ U*dx^5 -> 1.24e33 * sqrt(4e6/3e5) ~ 4.5e33) and dt halved. 20-day stability validated over the 16 corners of the (tau_rad, tau_drag, Phibar, DPhieq) prior box: 14/16 stable; the ONLY failures require simultaneously tau_rad=48h AND tau_drag=48h AND Phibar=8e6 (both damping channels nearly off + fastest waves). Solver failures there are soft-rejected by the NaN -> -1e30 likelihood guard; the corner is also data-excluded (tau_rad=48 h implies a near-flat phase curve vs the observed 4180 ppm amplitude). If a posterior ever piles up against those three upper bounds together, rerun with K6 scaled by sqrt(Phibar/4e6) and revalidate.
